meaning: n.恶言漫骂adj.漫骂的,非难的,恶言的
examples: Popular linguistic debate regularly deteriorates into invective and polemic.
He had expected criticism but not the invective which greeted his proposal.
Invective, to strengthen its shield of the new and the weak, and to enlarge the area?
Ms Gillard's 15 minutes of invective brought her more than 15 minutes of global fame.
That is the gist of some virtuoso invective by Hugh Trevor-Roper, a British historian who died in 2003.
